title: PostPilot
tagline: Social media scheduling and queue management for solo creators.
description: PostPilot is a lightweight social media scheduling tool built for solo creators and small teams — queue posts, manage drafts, and track scheduled content across platforms without the enterprise overhead.
story: Built for the creator who needs a queue, not a dashboard with 40 analytics panels. PostPilot is intentionally minimal — schedule it, forget it, move on.
journey: PostPilot came from a genuine personal need — I kept scheduling posts manually and losing track of drafts across platforms. Most scheduling tools are built for marketing teams and priced accordingly. I wanted something that covered the core loop (draft, schedule, publish) without the noise. It was also a good excuse to work through OAuth flows for multiple platforms in one project.
forWho: Solo creators, freelancers, and small teams who need post scheduling without the enterprise price tag or complexity.

detailedSpecs.architecture: Express API with a Bull job queue for scheduled publishing. React frontend for draft and queue management. PostgreSQL for post storage.
keyPatterns: Bull queue for reliable scheduled publishing
detailedSpecs.deployTarget: Any Node.js host with Redis for Bull
